summary: NATO's first Quantum Technologies Strategy, summarised in a 2024 official text, sets the end-state of transitioning Alliance cryptographic systems to quantum-safe cryptography and commits Allies and NATO bodies to develop and implement post-quantum cryptography and quantum key distribution. The NCI Agency and the NATO Cyber Security Centre have tested a hybrid post-quantum virtual private network and proposed it to the IETF for open standardisation, while Science for Peace and Security projects, Allied Command Transformation and the DIANA accelerator carry related work.
migrationTimeline: 2024 | NATO adopts its first Quantum Technologies Strategy, setting a quantum-safe end-state for Alliance cryptographic systems
govActors:
NATO | Adopts the Quantum Technologies Strategy committing Allies and NATO bodies to post-quantum cryptography and quantum key distribution
NCI Agency / NATO Cyber Security Centre | Tested a hybrid post-quantum VPN and proposed it to the IETF for open standardisation
NATO SPS | Science for Peace and Security Programme, funds post-quantum cryptography and quantum key distribution projects, including with Malta
NATO ACT | Allied Command Transformation, work on quantum-resistant cryptography for defence
NATO DIANA | Defence Innovation Accelerator for the North Atlantic, challenge featuring communications with integrated post-quantum cryptography

govActors (SPS): SPS project with Malta and the SPS quantum-technologies report (included results rows)
govActors (ACT): NATO Allied Command Transformation article on quantum-resistant cryptography for defence (included results row)
govActors (DIANA): DIANA Advanced Communication Technologies challenge featuring integrated post-quantum cryptography (included results row)
